stem: Maya purchased a vintage stamp on January 1. The function f(x) = 800(1.05)x, where 0 ≤ x ≤ 10, gives the predicted value, in dollars, of the stamp x years after Maya purchased it. What is the best interpretation of the statement “f(5) is approximately equal to 1,021” in this context?

answerOptions.content: 5 years after Maya purchased the vintage stamp, its predicted value is approximately 1,021 dollars.
When the stamp's predicted value is approximately 1,021 dollars, it is 5% greater than the predicted value, in dollars, on January 1 of the previous year.
When the stamp's predicted value is approximately 1,021 dollars, it is 5 times the predicted value, in dollars, on January 1 of the previous year.
From the day Maya purchased the stamp to 5 years after Maya purchased it, its predicted value increased by a total of approximately 1,021 dollars.

rationale: Choice A is correct. Since f(x) gives the predicted value of the stamp x years after purchase, f(5) is the predicted value 5 years after purchase. Saying f(5) is approximately 1,021 means the predicted value 5 years after purchase is approximately 1,021 dollars. Choices B and C misinterpret the meaning of the 5 and the growth factor. Choice D mistakes the function value for a total increase.
